etymology.description: Depicts an old man with long hair -> long
etymology.interpretation: The character 長 (cháng, "long") is a pictogram that represents an old man with long hair, suggesting the idea of something being drawn out or elongated, which leads to the meaning "long."

examples.chinese: 这条河很长(這條河很長)。
他有一头长发(他有一頭長髮)，看起来很优雅(看起來很優雅)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǒu yī tóu cháng fā, kàn qǐlái hěn yōuyǎ.
examples.english: He has long hair, which looks very elegant.

examples.chinese: 我的工作时间很长(我的工作時間很長)，需要更多的休息(需要更多的休息)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de gōngzuò shíjiān hěn cháng, xūyào gèng duō de xiūxi.
examples.english: My working hours are long, and I need more rest.
